page_content:
ENMA627 Nanotechnology Characterization (3 Credits) Techniques to characterize the properties of materials whose characteristic dimensions are a few to a few hundred nanometers, including "conventional" nanocrystalline materials, but concentrating on "novel" nanomaterials: carbon nanotubes, quantum dots, quantum wires, and quantum wells will be covered. The emphasis is on recent results from the scientiﬁc literature concerning those properties that make nanostructures interesting: quantum effects, novel transport phenomena, enhanced mechanical properties associated with localization and with small crystalline size. Credit Only Granted for: ENMA627 or ENME698T. Formerly: ENMA698T.

ENMA635 Wide Bandgap Materials and Devices (3 Credits) Presents the materials science of wide bandgap materials and analyzes the defects present in such materials from a device performance point of view. Jointly offered with: ENMA435. Restriction: Permission of ENGR-Materials Science & Engineering department. Credit Only Granted for: ENMA435 or ENMA635.
